task_completion.description: Open the patient record for John Zenon in GNU Health.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health interface should display the patient record or dashboard for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Document the acute toxic liver disease diagnosis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A condition, evaluation, or diagnosis entry showing 'Toxic liver disease' or an ICD-10 code starting with 'K71' in the patient's medical record.

task_completion.description: Log the adverse chemical exposure in the allergy/adverse reaction profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An entry in the patient's Allergies or Adverse Reactions section containing keywords like 'toluene', 'carbon tetrachloride', 'solvent', or 'degreaser'.

task_completion.description: Order a hepatic monitoring lab panel with a minimum of 3 liver function tests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A lab order or test request screen showing at least 3 distinct liver function tests (e.g., AST, ALT, Bilirubin, ALP) requested for the patient.
A prescription or medication order for an antiemetic drug such as 'Ondansetron', 'Promethazine', 'Metoclopramide', or 'Prochlorperazine'.

task_completion.description: Schedule a clinical reassessment follow-up in 3 to 7 days.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment scheduling screen or confirmation showing an appointment booked for John Zenon with a date that is 3 to 7 days after the current system date.

integrity.description: The agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop client being actively used, rather than terminal windows executing SQL commands or python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent entered the data into the actual application fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data should be visible within the proper form fields, dropdowns, and tables of the GNU Health application, not just typed into a text editor or search bar.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application UI to modify the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows showing psql commands, sqlite3 prompts, or database administration tools like pgAdmin.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is John Zenon. Diagnosis must be an ICD-10 code starting with K71 (Toxic liver disease). Allergy/adverse reaction must include at least one of: 'toluene', 'carbon tetrachloride', 'solvent', or 'degreaser'. Lab orders must include at least 3 liver function tests. Prescription must be an antiemetic such as Ondansetron, Promethazine, Metoclopramide, or Prochlorperazine. Follow-up appointment must be scheduled exactly 3 to 7 days from the current date.
